title: anthemis tinctoria var. discoidea
intro: Anthemis tinctoria var. discoidea, also known as the Yellow Chamomile, is a low-growing perennial plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a popular remedy for soothing skin irritations and reducing stress. The health benefits of Yellow Chamomile include calming the nervous system, reducing anxiety and insomnia, and relieving menstrual cramps and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Yellow Chamomile include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to create calming bath soaks, soothing skin creams, and relaxing aromatherapy blends.
